insight.headline: Storefront API update
insight.what_changed: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query. This change is available in Storefront API version 2026-10. The @inContext directive can be used to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel.
insight.developer_impact: Developers using the Storefront API should update their queries to include the channelId argument when necessary. This change enables more flexibility in querying data specific to different sales channels. Developers should review their existing queries and update them to take advantage of this new feature. The change is backwards compatible, so existing queries will still work, but using the channelId argument will provide more accurate results.
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants will benefit from more accurate and sales channel-specific data being displayed in their storefronts, leading to a better customer experience.
insight.why_it_matters: This update provides more flexibility and accuracy in querying sales channel-specific data, which is essential for merchants with multiple sales channels. It allows developers to build more robust and sales channel-aware applications.

also_worth_knowing.text: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate

updates.title: Physical inventory feature preview
updates.summary: The physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, allowing developers to build and test against new inventory APIs.
CLI version 4.5.2 fixes a warning when running theme commands in valid theme directories and bumps Shopify/theme-tools packages.

updates.title: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
updates.summary: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, affecting apps that store cardholder data.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: Hydrogen CLI version 13.0.2 adds non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and allows bypassing confirmation prompts in hydrogen upgrade.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: App Store requirement 4.1.2 mandates unique app names to reduce merchant confusion.

updates.title: Storefront API `@inContext` supports `channelId`
updates.summary: Storefront API version 2026-10 supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: The ExchangeLineItem type now includes productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle fields.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: POS UI Extensions API version 2026-07 includes discount allocations to bundle components.
CLI version 4.5.0 introduces new store commands, including listing and store creation.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.1
updates.summary: Hydrogen CLI version 13.0.1 reads disabled command metadata and preserves actionable build errors during deployment.

updates.title: @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38
updates.summary: Create Hydrogen version 5.0.38 adds an explicit app alias to new Hydrogen projects and uses jsconfig.json for JavaScript projects.

recap.headline: Shopify updates: new inventory APIs, mTLS certificate requirements, and Hydrogen improvements

themes.title: New Features and APIs
themes.detail: The physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, and the Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument. Additionally, new fields have been added to the ExchangeLineItem type, and POS UI Extensions API version 2026-07 includes discount allocations to bundle components.

themes.title: Security and Authentication
themes.detail: The card-deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, and apps that store cardholder data must be updated to use this certificate. Merchants should ensure their apps are compliant with this new requirement.

themes.title: Tooling and Development
themes.detail: The Shopify CLI has been updated to version 4.5.2, with improvements to theme commands and hydrogen link. Hydrogen has also been updated, with new features such as non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags, and improved error handling.

actions.text: Enable physical inventory feature preview on a development store
Update apps to use mTLS client certificate for card-deposit endpoint
Review and update app names to comply with new App Store requirements
